compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Pre-review clarification from epic PASS advisories f6b79c745225b705c/fa1f1ae29ce7f70dd: any indeterminate prerequisite record forces top-level unsigned INDETERMINATE; largest-window singleton exhaustion still emits exactly one indeterminate/evaluation-error record with detail=input-too-large before top-level remediation. This child will receive its normal sequential review later.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Remediated BLOCK review: replaced the G3/G4 container-harness extension with a dedicated prerequisite fidelity report and new opt-in eval; named and registered the prerequisite coverage contract; placed prerequisite_attribution_valid precisely as an na-default non-graded Binary veto; defined prompt isolation as captured-string absence checks; assigned record threading and completeness to plan_review_decide, the coverage flag to plan_review_coach, and unconditional unsigned INDETERMINATE escalation to orchestrator.finalize_verdict; added executable verification paths and defined tail-conflict.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Second remediation: added a dedicated focused Pass-2 verifier that receives each authoritative prerequisite plan block and cannot rewrite its expected ID; kept the general Binary and verifier untouched; made focused attribution a separate non-graded veto contract; added explicit decide input/output schema ownership; made ItemRecord additive with dedicated prerequisite ID fields while keeping the container harness unchanged; corrected test locations to existing repository structure.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Third remediation: restored the epic-approved existing optional index-keyed Pass-2 field and yes|no|na vocabulary; defined general and focused verification as separate non-overlapping index domains; each domain is reshaped and decided independently, then their decided lists are concatenated before the single existing partition call. Added named focused prompts, explicit coverage wrapper registration, Python-level focused-route completeness validation, and complete workflow/schema file impact. Retain prerequisite-indeterminate precedence over BLOCK because the certified epic explicitly requires any indeterminate prerequisite record to force whole-review unsigned INDETERMINATE.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Fourth remediation: added the exact opt-in module CLI command; explicitly assigned the prerequisite_indeterminate code change to this story in orchestrator.finalize_verdict; specified the coach input to local coverage dictionary to finalize_verdict data flow; confined the optional yes|no|na veto to plan_review_verification_model so code-review remains unchanged; defined canonical-ID lexical ordering, execution_review propagation, and prerequisite-aware finding IDs. The INDETERMINATE-before-BLOCK ordering is retained deliberately because the already-certified epic requires any indeterminate prerequisite record to force whole-review unsigned INDETERMINATE.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Fifth remediation: made the singleton focused finder a mandatory first vertical slice and stop gate; set absolute recall>=0.90, false-acceptance<=0.10, coverage=1.00, attribution-error=0.00 floors; require packed non-regression against the pinned singleton result; defined prerequisite_fidelity_report inputs, ParityReport output, formulas, metric keys, and failure behavior; defined pack_prerequisite_bins signature and exact token equation; changed finding identity to length-prefixed structured fields.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Sixth remediation: removed the conflicting pipe-delimited finding-ID construction; fixed the structured encoding to ordered UTF-8 fields with 4-byte big-endian length prefixes and SHA-256; added named production-YAML tests for the full indeterminate signal chain in both agentic and single-turn branches, including unsigned terminal verdict assertions and an exact proving command.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Seventh remediation: added a committed singleton baseline JSON artifact, exact write/read CLI commands, schema and corpus-digest validation, and automated comparative failures in prerequisite_fidelity_report. Also preserved legacy finding IDs byte-for-byte, scoped the versioned structured hash to new prerequisite findings, normalized INDETERMINATE verdict arrays by moving blockers to indeterminate, and documented prerequisite-free finalize_verdict call sites.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Eighth remediation: replaced all competing identity descriptions with one canonical four-field, length-prefixed prerequisite encoding; explicitly preserved the legacy pipe-delimited encoding for all non-prerequisite findings. Also documented reuse of the existing runner retry policy, structured indeterminate observability, and the parent-contract rationale for the plan-review-only optional verifier field.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Ninth remediation: defined the focused finder prompt file, system judgment rules, delimited subject/prerequisite input format, exact coverage and finding output obligations, and deterministic ownership of indeterminate results. Also specified focused-verifier context recovery and added named largest-window and observability tests with exact assertions.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Tenth remediation: added direct depends_on link to 75de and made closed-and-merged 75de/5967 delivery an execution precondition with an upstream contract test; reconciled the two slice commands; consolidated blocking-to-indeterminate array normalization in plan_review_decide and left finalize_verdict responsible only for the verdict string; defined PrerequisiteBlock; specified the focused verifier prompt file and exact attribution rules.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Eleventh remediation: defined singleton and baseline CLI modes and usage errors; replaced the stale undecorated Verification command with both real slice commands; registered prerequisite-consistency as blocking at 0.60 with the execution 0.80 floor; added schema golden verification, P8/drift regression coverage, coaching for reclassified findings, and the explicit verify_models kernel edit.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Twelfth remediation: placed prerequisite_attribution_valid unambiguously inside verification.binary with exact helper signatures and key path; named the existing finders batch as the sole coverage producer; defined PrerequisiteVerificationBlock and its packing formula; specified coachable filtering and all four YAML call sites; added the existing container eval and offline test as concrete compatibility oracles.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Remediated review findings f17ce70ba6939effc, f13b739f1cc02edc6, and fe9fdb8d2cc5f8c06. The focused verifier now has one canonical split algorithm (whole records, then a singleton findings tuple, never plan text); batch-step with: delivery is explicitly implemented through backward-compatible BatchRunRequest.with_inputs and interpreter resolution with named tests/files; and DET blockers now dominate prerequisite indeterminacy, yielding unsigned BLOCK with the coverage flag while no-DET indeterminacy yields unsigned INDETERMINATE. This precedence preserves known deterministic failures instead of masking them.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Remediated blocker f5c3df0429fdffa41 by preserving required original_index labels through verifier tuple splitting and index-keyed reassembly. For blocker f09050c2dfd6b2fbc, clarified that the singleton phase is non-releasable test scaffolding with no production consumer or independently closable outcome; splitting it would create an orphan contract/prompt task, so the approved leaf remains one atomic delivery with a fail-fast internal checkpoint. Also applied advisory feedback feb2d8720f9f4746d and fc03e74e5936078ea by restoring the epic-required single Pass-3 pipeline and specifying every decide/coach YAML data-flow edge. Realigned ff78c97ce9c3a3a0d to the certified epic: any prerequisite indeterminacy yields unsigned INDETERMINATE while DET blockers remain visible in the blocking array. Added exact model, prompt-front-matter, tail-conflict, seam, identity, and empty-indeterminate tests. Runtime rollback flag advice is declined: this story writes no migration/state and has a safe code-revert path; a flag would add an unapproved behavior gap.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS advisory dispositions: f081ce5a524161a67 is accepted as an implementation detail already implied by the generic batch with: seam—extend the v3 batch schema/schema builder to admit optional with: and cover it in test_batch_step.py; add the relevant schema file to the implementation change if required. f652d76e170761f79 is accepted—fidelity_spot_eval.py is extended, not newly created, and its __all__ must add prerequisite_packing_spot_eval and main. ff9d90b0f14bd229e is already covered by file impact for both named schema files. f460b63d97ac34965 is accepted for execution: add named lifecycle tests that exercise the real finder→verifier→decide→verdict handoffs with schema-valid stubbed model outputs. Remaining deferred advisories do not alter the signed design; atomicity, rollback, and rollout rationales are recorded in the plan.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Implementation handoff checkpoint: completed focused prerequisite coverage contract, stable finder/verifier packing primitives, plan-only attribution veto, sole finders-batch production routing from the preloaded relation snapshot, independent focused/general Pass-2 domains with one shared execution-aware Pass 3, prerequisite-indeterminate verdict propagation/coaching, structured finding identity, parity/CLI baseline, prompt/schema/YAML wiring, and metadata-only indeterminate observability. Happy-path oracle: 5/5 green. Selected focused/workflow/schema/lifecycle suites green (including 49-test focused batch and workflow set); ruff check src and mypy src/rebar green. No commit or push performed. Full make lint is blocked only by formatting of the orchestrator-owned untracked happy-path test, which implementation did not edit; one pre-existing kernel vocabulary assertion also expects the old plan-review Binary set and conflicts with this ticket's required plan-only attribution field.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Rebased implementation validated: focused prerequisite/workflow/schema suite 118 passed and make check passed. Broad non-integration run exposed one missing CONTRACT_SCHEMAS registration for the new prerequisite verifier pair; the existing schema-coverage oracle was RED, registration was added, and the focused regression set is now 17 passed. Amended commit 0f284b4ab (same Change-Id I4b52adda9318e3471bb15b426e34a4f79801afa4) pushed as Gerrit change 899: https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/899. Broad non-integration rerun remains active.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart

All three existing RED oracles were remediated; focused regression set is 26 passed and make check is GREEN. A teardown guard error was diagnostic-only: HEAD changed because the commit was amended during the run. Final patchset commit f4dc5cf17 pushed to Gerrit 899; a clean broad rerun will run with HEAD held fixed.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart

Local make check and focused regression suites are also GREEN. Awaiting Gerrit change 899 LLM-Review +1, Verified +1, and zero unresolved comments.

The verifier output is the authoritative gap list. A new continuation change will be created from current origin/main; merged 899 remains regression-safe (local 6951 pass, CI +1, LLM +1).

CLI baseline validation supplies scalar comparison metrics and returns exit 1 on a seeded packed regression. Baseline JSON now carries atomic recall, false_accept, coverage completeness, attribution error rate, model, corpus digest, and recorded timestamp. Prior held-out suite evidence remains 123 passed with the comparative-regression mutation correctly RED.

Local evidence: make check green; affected suite 62 passed; broad non-integration/non-external suite 6,980 passed, 28 skipped; prior live singleton and packed comparative runs both recall 1.0, false acceptance 0.0. compiled_state.description:
# Add focused prerequisite consistency review

## Goal
Evaluate whether a ticket plan remains consistent with each direct prerequisite without injecting prerequisite material into general reviewers or changing existing parent and child review behavior.

## Upstream contract
Execution precondition: do not claim or implement this story until direct dependencies `75de-02b0-d454-410b` and `5967-4476-7f9f-442d` are closed and their reviewed changes are merged into the feature branch. The dependency links enforce that order. This plan targets their certified planned contracts: `75de` must deliver the review-time `PlanRelationSnapshot` preload keyed by authoritative canonical ID with relation kind, readable state, pinned material, and plan text; `5967` must deliver the review-phase snapshot plus `pass3_over_findings(..., execution_review=...)`. At implementation start, verify both symbols and contract tests exist; if either differs, stop as upstream contract drift rather than defining a local stub or duplicating ownership. This story neither recollects relations nor changes parent and child context handling.

## Implementation
- Register one prerequisite-consistency criterion. It runs only when the shared `PlanRelationSnapshot` preload succeeded and contains readable direct `depends_on` or inverse `blocks` prerequisites. The same canonical ID reached through both relation forms is evaluated once.
- Define stable order as ascending lexicographic order of authoritative canonical ticket ID strings. Use it for prerequisite packing, local finding flattening, split halves, and tail-conflict fixtures.
- Add `PASS_PREREQUISITE_FINDER = "plan-review-prerequisite-finder"` and `PASS_PREREQUISITE_VERIFIER = "plan-review-prerequisite-verifier"` in `passes.py`. Extend the production Pass-1 batch runner with the prerequisite-only finder route. It receives the subject plan plus stable whole prerequisite blocks and invokes `plan_review_prerequisite_coverage`. General criterion requests retain their existing inputs.
- Define measurable isolation: capture every request and assert that each general finder, general verifier, and coach system prompt, user prompt, and instructions contain neither prerequisite canonical IDs nor prerequisite plan text. Assert exactly one relation preload supplies both pin creation and prerequisite routing. Only the two focused prerequisite prompts may receive prerequisite material.
- Define `_prerequisite_coverage_model()` in `passes.py` with `PrerequisiteFinderFinding`, a new Pydantic model matching existing Pass-1 fields exactly: `finding: str`, `criteria: list[str]` constrained to `prerequisite-consistency`, `location: str = ""`, `evidence: list[str] = []`, `scenarios: list[str] = []`, `impact: str = ""`, `checklist_item: str = ""`, `suggested_fix: str = ""`, and required `prerequisite_id: str`. Define outer `PrerequisiteCoverageOutput { records: list[PrerequisiteCoverageRecord] }`; each record is `{prerequisite_id: canonical string, disposition: consistent|finding|indeterminate, findings: list[PrerequisiteFinderFinding], reason_code?: evaluation-error|output-invalid|attribution-invalid, detail?: string}`. The nested finding ID must equal its enclosing record ID. Add `contracts.register_contract("plan_review_prerequisite_coverage", _prerequisite_coverage_model)` to the existing `passes.register_contracts()` function.
- Require exactly one record per readable pinned canonical ID. `consistent` requires no findings or reason code. `finding` requires findings carrying that canonical `prerequisite_id` and no reason code. `indeterminate` requires no findings and one closed-enum reason code. Missing, duplicate, unknown-ID, parse-invalid, or schema-invalid output maps unresolved IDs in the bin to `output-invalid`; provider failure maps them to `evaluation-error`. Indeterminate records never assert incompatibility.
- Keep batching semantically neutral. Each focused Pass-1 prompt asks for independent subject-to-one-prerequisite judgments and forbids cross-prerequisite findings. Stable bins contain whole blocks only. Neither subject nor prerequisite is split. A tail conflict means the only incompatible prerequisite is the lexicographically last canonical ID in a multi-prerequisite packed bin; it must be detected and attributed identically to the same prerequisite evaluated in a singleton call.
- Reuse the registered index-keyed `plan_review_verification` contract for focused Pass 2 and place `prerequisite_attribution_valid` inside its `binary` object. Change `_build_binary(forbid, *, extra_fields: dict[str, tuple[type, Any]] | None = None) -> type`; after building existing fields, merge `extra_fields or {}` into the `create_model` field mapping. Change `_build_verification_output(forbid, severity_cls, *, binary_extra_fields: dict[str, tuple[type, Any]] | None = None) -> type` to call `_build_binary(forbid, extra_fields=binary_extra_fields)`. Only `plan_review_verification_model` passes `binary_extra_fields={"prerequisite_attribution_valid": (str, Field(default="na", description="yes|no|na"))}`. Base and code-review callers omit the keyword and remain byte-identical. Do not add the field to `_BINARY_NA_DEFAULT` or `GRADED_BINARY`; the focused decide path reads `verif["binary"].get("prerequisite_attribution_valid", "na")` as a veto outside validity arithmetic.
- The focused verifier receives the subject plan and, for each local finding, its authoritative prerequisite ID and plan block from the preload. It emits the existing `{index, severity_attributes, binary}` entry. Set attribution `yes` only when the finding is supported by that exact pair, `no` for wrong or unprovable attribution, and never `na` for a focused finding. Multiple whole focused records may share a call within budget.
- Preserve two non-overlapping verification index domains. General arrays retain `0..G-1`. Flatten focused findings in stable `(prerequisite_id, finding_index)` order into local `0..P-1` and verify only that list. Never inject focused findings into general arrays.
- In `plan_review_decide`, reshape the general verification domain with its existing logic and reshape the focused local domain separately so neither domain can shift the other. For the focused domain, missing, duplicate, malformed, out-of-range, `no`, or `na` attribution converts that prerequisite record to `indeterminate/attribution-invalid` and removes its incompatibility findings. After both verification domains are validated, offset each surviving focused finding and verification index by `G`, concatenate aligned general and focused arrays, and call `pass3_over_findings(..., execution_review=execution_review)` exactly once. Determine `execution_review` once from the review-phase snapshot supplied by `5967`; this single shared Pass 3 applies the 0.8 execution floor to every finding. There is no parallel decide pipeline.
- Partition the one returned decided array with DET findings through the existing single `partition_findings` call. Upheld focused findings therefore surface in blocking or advisory arrays with canonical `prerequisite_id` without shifting general indices. Extend `mint_finding_id` to use the authoritative structured identity encoding defined in Binding implementation sequence step 5 whenever `prerequisite_id` is present; identical text from different prerequisites cannot deduplicate.
- Size the actual focused prompt topology, including system prompt, subject plan, prerequisite or finding blocks, output reserve, and headroom, through a prerequisite-specific adapter in `sizing.py`. On context-limit error, split a stable bin into contiguous halves recursively. Escalate a singleton through `models_at_or_above()`. Largest-window exhaustion emits `indeterminate/evaluation-error` with `detail=input-too-large`. Non-context provider failure emits that record for every unresolved ID.
- Add optional `has_prerequisites`, `prerequisite_coverage`, `prerequisite_findings`, and `prerequisite_verifications` properties to `plan_review_decide_input.schema.json`; do not use conditional schema requirements. Both YAML branches always pass false and empty arrays when the route does not apply. Python validation inside `plan_review_decide` requires complete arrays when `has_prerequisites=true`. Extend decide output and coach input schemas with final coverage records.
- Wire focused finder and verifier steps and arrays through both branches in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml`. All four `plan_review_decide` YAML `with:` blocks pass `has_prerequisites`, `${{ steps.finders.outputs.prerequisite_coverage }}`, focused findings, and focused verifications. `plan_review_decide_output.schema.json` returns the validated final `prerequisite_coverage`. All four `plan_review_coach_inputs` YAML `with:` blocks pass `${{ steps.decide.outputs.indeterminate }}` and `${{ steps.decide.outputs.prerequisite_coverage }}`; its output schema preserves that coverage, and all four `plan_review_coach` step `with:` blocks pass it as the direct `prerequisite_coverage` input. In `workflow_ops.plan_review_coach`, compute `prerequisite_indeterminate = any(record.get("disposition") == "indeterminate" for record in ctx.inputs.get("prerequisite_coverage", []))`, add that boolean to the existing local `coverage` dictionary, and pass the same dictionary through the existing `coverage=coverage` argument to `orchestrator.finalize_verdict`. No op rereads the store.
- This story explicitly modifies both decision assembly and terminal verdict while preserving the epic's precedence. In `plan_review_decide`, after the single `partition_findings` call, if any final prerequisite coverage record is indeterminate, retain every `tier == "DET"` entry in `parts["blocking"]` so known structural failures stay visible; move only non-DET blockers into `parts["indeterminate"]` with `decision=indeterminate` and `reason=prerequisite-coverage-indeterminate`. Return those normalized parts plus coverage. In `orchestrator.finalize_verdict`, evaluate `coverage.get("prerequisite_indeterminate")` before blocking: any indeterminate prerequisite produces top-level unsigned `INDETERMINATE`, even when retained DET blockers remain in the blocking array. The finalizer does not move arrays. This preserves complete diagnostic evidence without allowing incomplete prerequisite coverage to become BLOCK or signed PASS.
- Add optional `gold_prerequisite_id` and `pred_prerequisite_id` fields to `parity.ItemRecord`, both defaulting to `None`. Preserve existing fields and container behavior. Add `prerequisite_fidelity_report` for authoritative-ID attribution plus recall and false acceptance. Existing container corpus results remain unchanged.
- Add separate opt-in `prerequisite_packing_spot_eval` and a module CLI entry point `--prerequisite-packing` in `fidelity_spot_eval.py`; do not route prerequisite data through `packing_spot_eval` or `container_fidelity_report`. Add eight or more gold sets of at least three prerequisites: two all-consistent, two single-conflict, two tail-conflict, one attribution-confusion, and one multi-bin. Packed recall may not decrease, false acceptance may not increase, coverage must be complete, and attribution errors must be zero. Failure retains smaller bins or singleton calls.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] General finder, general verifier, and coach requests contain no prerequisite IDs or plan text.
- [ ] One relation preload supplies both material-pin creation and prerequisite routing.
- [ ] Batch `with:` expressions resolve into `BatchRunRequest.with_inputs`; old batch steps default to an empty mapping unchanged.
- [ ] Existing parent and child criteria retain existing container corpus results.
- [ ] Stable focused batching never splits the subject or a prerequisite block.
- [ ] Every readable pinned prerequisite receives exactly one closed-schema disposition.
- [ ] The plan-review-only Pass-2 veto uses `yes|no|na`; code-review schemas remain unchanged.
- [ ] General and focused index domains are independently reshaped and cannot shift each other.
- [ ] Missing or incorrect attribution cannot produce a blocking finding.
- [ ] Every upheld focused finding appears in the verdict with its authoritative prerequisite ID.
- [ ] Same-text findings from different prerequisite IDs retain distinct finding IDs.
- [ ] Every indeterminate record contains no incompatibility finding.
- [ ] Any indeterminate prerequisite forces top-level unsigned INDETERMINATE; concurrent DET blockers remain visibly retained in the blocking array and `coverage.prerequisite_indeterminate=true`.
- [ ] Largest-window exhaustion emits `indeterminate/evaluation-error` with `detail=input-too-large`.
- [ ] The opt-in prerequisite evaluation meets recall, false-acceptance, coverage, and attribution thresholds.

## Verification
- Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review.py tests/unit/test_plan_review_prerequisites.py tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_workflow.py tests/unit/workflow/test_batch_step.py tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py`.
- Named structural tests include `test_mint_finding_id_different_prerequisite_ids_produce_distinct_ids` and `test_indeterminate_coverage_records_have_no_findings`.
- Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/interfaces/contracts/test_plan_review_prerequisite_isolation.py tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py`.
- Run `.venv/bin/python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.fidelity_spot_eval --prerequisite-packing --singleton --write-baseline src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-prerequisite-packing.baseline.json`; require exit 0 and record the singleton metrics. Then run `.venv/bin/python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.fidelity_spot_eval --prerequisite-packing --baseline src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-prerequisite-packing.baseline.json`; require exit 0 with no absolute or comparative regression failures and record the packed metrics.
- Run `make check` and `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -m "not integration"`.

## Binding implementation sequence and evaluation contract
The following details replace the earlier shorthand about relative fidelity and define the delivery order:

1. Internal proof phase: implement the coverage contract, focused finder prompt, singleton runner, gold corpus, and opt-in CLI as test scaffolding. Run the singleton gold evaluation before batching or production workflow wiring. Do not continue until singleton recall is at least 0.90, singleton false-acceptance is at most 0.10, coverage completeness is 1.00, and prerequisite attribution error rate is 0.00; record the first green output as the pinned baseline. This checkpoint is deliberately non-releasable: it has no production route, user-facing behavior, supported public surface, or independently closable acceptance outcome, and the prompt/contract must not land orphaned from their sole workflow consumer.
2. Production completion phase: implement packed finder calls and require packed recall to be at least the pinned singleton recall and packed false-acceptance to be no greater than the pinned singleton value, while retaining the absolute 0.90 and 0.10 floors, 1.00 coverage, and 0.00 attribution error. Only after that gate passes implement focused Pass 2, the single shared Pass-3 integration, and terminal verdict enforcement. The story is one atomic delivery and is neither closed nor released until both phases, all acceptance criteria, and all verification commands pass; the phase boundary is a fail-fast implementation checkpoint, not a child-ticket delivery boundary.
3. Define `prerequisite_fidelity_report(baseline: Sequence[ItemRecord], candidate: Sequence[ItemRecord], *, min_recall: float = 0.90, max_false_accept: float = 0.10, min_gold: int = 20) -> ParityReport` in `src/rebar/llm/parity.py`. It calls `parity_report(baseline, candidate, min_gold=min_gold)`, reads candidate recall and false acceptance using `_recall_false_accept`, and adds failures when candidate recall is below `min_recall`, candidate false acceptance exceeds `max_false_accept`, any authoritative readable ID lacks exactly one record, or any caught finding has `pred_prerequisite_id != gold_prerequisite_id`. Its metrics add `coverage_completeness`, `prerequisite_attribution_error_rate`, and the four configured floors. `passed` is true only when the base report and every added check pass. The CLI prints those exact metric keys and exits 1 on failure.
4. Define `pack_prerequisite_bins(blocks: Sequence[PrerequisiteBlock], *, subject_plan: str, system_prompt: str, model: str | None, per_block_output_tokens: int = PER_FINDING_VERIFY_TOKENS, headroom: float = DEFAULT_VERIFY_WINDOW_HEADROOM) -> tuple[list[list[PrerequisiteBlock]], list[PrerequisiteBlock]]` in `sizing.py`. Order blocks lexicographically by canonical ID. Compute `limit = int(largest_window_tokens(model) * headroom)`. Compute fixed input as `est_tokens(system_prompt) + est_tokens(subject_plan) + VERIFY_SYSTEM_RESERVE_TOKENS`. Charge each whole block `est_tokens(block.rendered_text) + per_block_output_tokens`. Greedily append while fixed plus the sum of block charges is at most limit; return a block as oversized when fixed plus its charge exceeds limit alone. Runtime context-limit splitting and model escalation then apply to these bins.
5. For prerequisite-scoped findings, use the single canonical structured identity encoding defined in Binding signal-chain and identity tests; do not define another encoding here. Non-prerequisite findings retain the legacy identity path unchanged.

The first-slice proving command is `.venv/bin/python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.fidelity_spot_eval --prerequisite-packing --singleton --write-baseline src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-prerequisite-packing.baseline.json`. The second-slice proving command is `.venv/bin/python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.fidelity_spot_eval --prerequisite-packing --baseline src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-prerequisite-packing.baseline.json`. Record both results on this ticket before proceeding.

## Binding signal-chain and identity tests
- The single canonical prerequisite-scoped identity encoding is exact: encode four fields as UTF-8, prefix each with a 4-byte big-endian unsigned byte length, and concatenate them in this order: fixed version string `prerequisite-finding-id-v1`, finding text, comma-joined sorted criteria, and authoritative prerequisite ID. Hash the concatenated bytes with SHA-256 and retain the existing ID truncation. This encoding is used only when `prerequisite_id` is present. Every non-prerequisite finding continues to use the existing pipe-delimited encoding byte-for-byte.
- Add `test_prerequisite_indeterminate_signal_chain_agentic` and `test_prerequisite_indeterminate_signal_chain_single_turn` to `tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_workflow.py`. Each loads the production `plan-review.yaml`, supplies a fake focused decide result containing one indeterminate prerequisite record and no DET blocker, follows its respective verifier branch, asserts the coach input receives that exact record, asserts the coverage dictionary passed to `finalize_verdict` contains `prerequisite_indeterminate=True`, and asserts the terminal verdict is `INDETERMINATE` with `signature.signed=false`. Add `test_prerequisite_indeterminate_retains_det_blocker` with a DET blocker plus prerequisite indeterminacy and assert unsigned `INDETERMINATE`, the DET finding remains in blocking, and the coverage flag remains true.
- Acceptance criterion: both production-YAML signal-chain tests pass, proving neither workflow branch can silently drop prerequisite coverage before final verdict assembly.
- Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_workflow.py -k prerequisite_indeterminate_signal_chain` and require two passing tests.

## Binding comparative baseline and compatibility behavior
- The first-slice command is `.venv/bin/python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.fidelity_spot_eval --prerequisite-packing --singleton --write-baseline src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-prerequisite-packing.baseline.json`. It exits nonzero unless the absolute floors pass, then atomically writes JSON keys `recall`, `false_accept`, `coverage_completeness`, `prerequisite_attribution_error_rate`, `model`, `corpus_digest`, and `recorded_at`. The generated baseline is committed and its four metrics are copied to the ticket comment.
- Extend `prerequisite_fidelity_report` with `baseline_recall: float | None = None` and `baseline_false_accept: float | None = None`. When supplied, add gating failures when candidate recall is below `baseline_recall` or candidate false acceptance exceeds `baseline_false_accept`, in addition to all absolute floors.
- The second-slice command is `.venv/bin/python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.fidelity_spot_eval --prerequisite-packing --baseline src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-prerequisite-packing.baseline.json`. It validates the baseline schema and corpus digest, passes both baseline metrics into `prerequisite_fidelity_report`, and exits nonzero on absolute or comparative regression. Acceptance criterion: this command exits 0 and its report contains no `recall regressed from singleton` or `false acceptance regressed from singleton` failure.
- Preserve `mint_finding_id` byte-for-byte for every finding without `prerequisite_id`; all existing stored IDs remain valid. New prerequisite-scoped findings use only the canonical four-field encoding defined in Binding signal-chain and identity tests. No migration or dual write is required because prerequisite-scoped findings do not exist before this feature.
- When prerequisite coverage is indeterminate, use only the normalized decision and finalizer behavior defined in the Implementation section; there is no second array-mutation site. The resulting verdict arrays and string remain consistent while retaining all findings for remediation and UI display.
- The P8 short-circuit and `drift_refresh` call sites pass no prerequisite coverage by design; `coverage.get("prerequisite_indeterminate")` is false on those paths and their existing verdicts remain unchanged.

## Binding operational behavior
- Focused finder and verifier calls use the existing runner retry classification and exponential-backoff policy for rate limits and retryable 5xx failures. Only exhausted retries map unresolved IDs to `indeterminate/evaluation-error`; context-limit failures continue through split and model escalation.
- Emit structured event `plan_review_prerequisite_indeterminate` with ticket ID, prerequisite ID, reason code, model, attempt count, and bin size for every evaluation-error, output-invalid, or attribution-invalid record. Increment counters partitioned by reason code; never include prerequisite plan text.
- Reusing `plan_review_verification` is deliberate because the certified epic requires the optional attribution field in the existing index-keyed Pass-2 contract and the same threshold pipeline. The field is added only by `plan_review_verification_model`; code-review and kernel verification contracts remain unchanged.

## Binding focused prompt and failure contracts
- Create `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_prerequisite_finder.md`, resolved by prompt ID `plan-review-prerequisite-finder`. Its YAML front matter is `schema_version: 1`, title `Plan-review prerequisite finder`, description of isolated prerequisite consistency Pass 1, `outputs: plan_review_prerequisite_coverage`, `execution_mode: single_turn`, and `category: plan-review-pass`, matching the prompt-library contract used by existing reviewers. Its system instructions say: compare the subject plan independently with each supplied prerequisite plan; report only a concrete incompatibility where the subject depends on an output, interface, ordering, invariant, acceptance condition, or file-impact assumption that the prerequisite plan contradicts or cannot deliver. Do not review either plan for general quality, infer transitive prerequisites, compare prerequisites with each other, or create a finding merely because details are absent. Use only supplied text and canonical IDs.
- The finder user input is framed as `SUBJECT_PLAN` followed by stable repeated blocks `PREREQUISITE id=<authoritative-id>` and `PLAN_TEXT`, with explicit begin/end delimiters. The prompt states that block position has no semantic meaning and each judgment must be independent of other blocks in the call.
- The prompt must return `PrerequisiteCoverageOutput` with exactly one record for every supplied canonical ID and no other IDs. `consistent` means no concrete incompatibility was found and has empty findings. `finding` means one or more concrete incompatibilities were found; each finding uses the existing Pass-1 finding fields `finding`, `criteria`, `location`, `evidence`, `scenarios`, `impact`, `checklist_item`, and `suggested_fix`, plus required `prerequisite_id` equal to the enclosing record ID. The only criterion ID is the registered prerequisite-consistency criterion. The model never emits `indeterminate`; runner and schema-validation failures create indeterminate records deterministically.
- Focused verifier context-limit recovery has one authoritative algorithm. Before packing, each focused finding carries required `original_index`, its position in the authoritative flat `0..P-1` focused domain. First split bins between whole `PrerequisiteVerificationBlock` records into stable contiguous halves. If one record alone is oversized, split only that record's `findings` tuple into stable contiguous halves while repeating the complete subject plan and complete prerequisite plan text in each call; never split either plan text. Every split-call prompt labels each entry with its unchanged `original_index` and requires the verifier to echo that value as output `index`; reassembly merges by echoed index before reshape and attribution validation, never by half-call position. Escalate a single-finding record through `models_at_or_above()`. If that single-finding record fails at the largest window, convert the whole prerequisite record to `indeterminate/evaluation-error` with `detail=input-too-large` and discard every partial verifier result for that prerequisite.
- Add `test_largest_window_exhaustion_emits_indeterminate` to `tests/unit/test_plan_review_prerequisites.py`; mock every model to return a context-limit error and assert the exact disposition, reason code, and detail for both finder and verifier paths.
- Add acceptance coverage for observability: tests assert each evaluation-error, output-invalid, and attribution-invalid record emits `plan_review_prerequisite_indeterminate` with ticket ID, prerequisite ID, reason code, model, attempt count, and bin size; counters are partitioned by reason; captured events contain no prerequisite plan text.
- Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_prerequisites.py -k "largest_window_exhaustion or indeterminate_observability"` and require all selected tests to pass.

## Binding prerequisite block and verifier prompt
- Define `@dataclass(frozen=True) class PrerequisiteBlock` in `sizing.py` with `canonical_id: str`, `relation_kind: str`, and `rendered_text: str`. Construct `rendered_text` exactly as the finder block delimiters plus the upstream snapshot plan text; pinned hashes remain metadata and are not inserted into the LLM prompt. Finder, verifier, token estimation, stable sorting, and split recovery use this same type.
- Create `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_prerequisite_verifier.md`, resolved by `PASS_PREREQUISITE_VERIFIER = "plan-review-prerequisite-verifier"`. Its YAML front matter is `schema_version: 1`, title `Plan-review prerequisite verifier`, description of isolated prerequisite attribution verification, `outputs: plan_review_verification`, `execution_mode: single_turn`, and `category: plan-review-pass`. Its system instructions evaluate each indexed finding only against the supplied subject plan and its single authoritative `PrerequisiteBlock`; set `prerequisite_attribution_valid=yes` only when the evidence establishes that exact pair causes the claimed incompatibility, `no` when another prerequisite or unsupported inference is required, and never `na` for focused entries. It must preserve the input index and authoritative ID, cannot create findings, and cannot compare records with each other.
- At implementation start run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_prerequisites.py -k upstream_contract` to assert the merged `PlanRelationSnapshot` fields and execution-review call signature match this plan before any focused-route code is changed.

## Binding criterion and CLI registration
- Register criterion ID `prerequisite-consistency` with `default_posture: blocking`, `block_threshold: 0.60`, and `exec: 1-TURN`. A verified incompatibility at or above 0.60 blocks in planning mode; the shared execution-review path applies its 0.80 floor after the ticket enters execution mode.
- Add a new module CLI from scratch in `fidelity_spot_eval.py` using `main(argv)` plus `if __name__ == "__main__": raise SystemExit(main())`. `--prerequisite-packing` selects the prerequisite corpus. `--singleton` bypasses `pack_prerequisite_bins` and issues exactly one focused-finder call per prerequisite block; it is valid only with `--write-baseline <path>`. `--baseline <path>` runs packed calls, loads the pinned singleton JSON, and enforces absolute plus comparative gates. Supplying both modes or neither baseline mode is a usage error with exit 2.
- Extend `plan_review_coach` coachable inputs with indeterminate findings whose reason is `prerequisite-coverage-indeterminate`, so reclassified blockers retain remediation notes.
- Add acceptance tests proving P8 short-circuit and `drift_refresh` omit prerequisite coverage and retain existing verdicts.
- Explicit kernel edit target: `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify_models.py` implements the exact `_build_binary(..., extra_fields=...)` and `_build_verification_output(..., binary_extra_fields=...)` signatures defined in Implementation; only `plan_review_verification_model` supplies the mapping, and base plus code-review models remain unchanged.
- Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/interfaces/contracts/test_workflow_schema_golden.py` after schema updates and require it to pass.

## Binding workflow production and verifier batching
- The existing YAML `finders` batch step is the sole producer of `prerequisite_coverage`. `precheck` exposes the single upstream `relation_snapshot`; `finders` receives it in `with:` alongside routed criteria. Implement that delivery by extending `BatchRunRequest` in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/runners.py` with `with_inputs: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)`, and by updating `src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py::_run_batch` to resolve `step.get("with", {})` through the normal expression resolver and pass the resulting mapping as `with_inputs`. `ProductionBatchRunner` reads `req.with_inputs["relation_snapshot"]`; it does not reread the store. Existing batch YAML without `with:` constructs the default empty mapping and retains byte-for-byte behavior at the runner boundary. When `prerequisite-consistency` is included, `ProductionBatchRunner` converts that snapshot to stable `PrerequisiteBlock` bins, runs `PASS_PREREQUISITE_FINDER`, and returns both existing `findings` and new `prerequisite_coverage`. When the criterion is absent it returns an empty coverage array. Every downstream branch references `${{ steps.finders.outputs.prerequisite_coverage }}`; no second snapshot read or coverage producer exists. Add interpreter seam tests proving `with:` resolution and compatibility tests proving old batch requests omit inputs and remain unchanged.
- In `sizing.py`, define `@dataclass(frozen=True) class PrerequisiteVerificationBlock` with `canonical_id: str`, `findings: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]`, and `rendered_text: str`. Define `pack_prerequisite_verifier_bins(records: Sequence[PrerequisiteVerificationBlock], *, subject_plan: str, system_prompt: str, model: str | None, per_finding_output_tokens: int = PER_FINDING_VERIFY_TOKENS, headroom: float = DEFAULT_VERIFY_WINDOW_HEADROOM) -> tuple[list[list[PrerequisiteVerificationBlock]], list[PrerequisiteVerificationBlock]]`. Sort by canonical ID. Use the same fixed-input calculation as finder packing; charge each whole record `est_tokens(rendered_text) + per_finding_output_tokens * len(findings)`. Greedily pack while total is within `int(largest_window_tokens(model) * headroom)` and return records oversized alone. Recursive recovery may split an oversized records finding tuple, but never splits subject or prerequisite plan text.
- In `plan_review_decide`, non-DET copied blockers receive `reason=prerequisite-coverage-indeterminate` before entering `parts["indeterminate"]`; DET blockers stay in blocking. Pass `indeterminate: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.indeterminate }}` and `prerequisite_coverage: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.prerequisite_coverage }}` into `plan_review_coach_inputs` in all four YAML call sites. In `plan_review_coach_inputs`, compute `reclassified = [f for f in indeterminate if f.get("reason") == "prerequisite-coverage-indeterminate"]`, set `coachable = blocking + surviving + reclassified`, and preserve prerequisite coverage for the subsequent coach op; the coach consumes that list without inspecting unrelated indeterminate findings.
- Container compatibility proving command: run `.venv/bin/python -m rebar prompt eval plan-review-container` and `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k container`; require both exit 0 and the existing tolerance values in `plan-review-container.eval.yaml` to remain unchanged.

## Binding review corrections
- Atomicity rationale: the internal singleton proof is test scaffolding only and cannot be released, enabled, or accepted independently; the production route is its sole consumer. Splitting would create an orphaned prompt/contract ticket rather than a coherent user-visible increment. The evidence gate remains an in-ticket fail-fast checkpoint.
- Back-out is a code revert of this atomic story: it adds no migration, first-order field, or independently written state. Failed/incomplete focused coverage is unsigned, so reverting the route restores the preceding workflow without rewriting attestations. A separate runtime flag would create an unreviewed behavior gap and is not part of the approved epic rollout.
- The focused verifier recovery algorithm above is authoritative: split whole records first, then only an oversized singleton record's findings tuple; the subject and prerequisite plan text are always repeated whole. Tests are named `test_batching_never_splits_subject_or_prerequisite_block` and `test_focused_general_index_domains_independent`.
- `PlanRelationSnapshot` preload unavailability disables focused prerequisite routing and produces the preload failure behavior owned by the upstream snapshot story; this story never performs an ad hoc relation read.
- Batch `with:` data is an explicit generic workflow-engine input seam, not YAML metadata ignored by the runner. `tests/unit/workflow/test_batch_step.py` covers resolution/default compatibility and `tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py` covers snapshot consumption without a store reread.
- Verdict precedence matches the certified epic: any indeterminate prerequisite yields unsigned INDETERMINATE, while retained DET blockers remain visible in the blocking array. Tests cover both no-DET and concurrent-DET signal chains.

compiled_state.title: Review direct prerequisites with a focused token-budget-batched criterion
